sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMP, value NUMERIC(10, 2));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at, value) VALUES (1, 'Test A', NOW(), 10.50), (2, 'Test B', NOW(), NULL), (3, '', NOW(), 5), (4, 'Test C', NOW(), 100.12345);
CREATE TABLE second_table (ref_id INT, description VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO second_table (ref_id, description) VALUES (1, 'Detail A'), (1, 'Detail B'), (2, NULL), (3, 'Detail C');
SELECT id, "truncate"(name, 3) AS short_name FROM test_table WHERE value >= (SELECT AVG(value) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL);

SELECT A.id, "truncate"(A.name || ' ' || COALESCE(B.description, 'No Description'), 20) AS full_name FROM test_table A LEFT JOIN second_table B ON A.id = B.ref_id WHERE A.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '1 DAY';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT "truncate"(name, 10) AS truncated_name FROM test_table GROUP BY truncated_name HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1) subquery;

SELECT id, value, "truncate"(description, 15) AS brief_desc FROM (SELECT A.id, A.value, B.description FROM test_table A JOIN second_table B ON A.id = B.ref_id WHERE A.value >= 0) AS combined_query ORDER BY value DESC;
SELECT id, "truncate"(name, 0) FROM test_table WHERE id >= (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table WHERE name <> '');
SELECT "truncate"(name, 5) AS truncated_name FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL OR value >= 5.0;
